tab1.title: Details
tab1.content:
• Ash gourd is a large fruit vegetable with a waxy outer layer.
  • It has a mild taste and high water content.
  • Used in curries, soups, and sweets.
  • Popular in Indian cuisine.
  • Known for its cooling properties.

tab2.title: Benefits
tab2.content:
• Hydrates the body
  • Supports digestion
  • Helps in weight management

tab1.title: Details
tab1.content:
• Bitter gourd is a green vegetable known for its strong bitter taste.
  • Widely used in Indian and Asian cooking.
  • It has a rough, textured surface.
  • Used in curries, stir-fries, and juices.
  • Highly valued for medicinal properties.

tab1.title: Details
tab1.content:
• Bottle gourd is a light green vegetable with high water content.
  • It has a mild taste.
  • Used in curries and soups.
  • Popular in Indian cooking.
  • Easy to digest.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: • Also used to make utensils in ancient times.

tab1.title: Details
tab1.content:
• Brinjal is a widely used vegetable also known as eggplant.
  • It comes in various shapes and colors.
  • Used in curries, roasted dishes, and fried recipes.
  • It has a soft texture when cooked.
  • Popular worldwide.

tab1.title: Details
tab1.content:
• Capsicum is a colorful vegetable available in red, green, and yellow varieties.
  • It has a sweet and mild flavor.
  • Used in salads, stir-fries, and pizzas.
  • Rich in vitamins.
  • Widely used in global cuisine.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: • Low in calories and high in nutrients.

tab1.title: Details
tab1.content:
• Chayote is a light green fruit vegetable with a mild taste.
  • It has a crisp texture.
  • Used in salads and cooked dishes.
  • Popular in Latin American cuisine.
  • Low in calories.

tab1.title: Details
tab1.content:
• Chili pepper is a spicy fruit vegetable used worldwide.
  • It adds heat and flavor to dishes.
  • Available in many varieties.
  • Used fresh or dried.
  • Essential in many cuisines.

tab1.title: Details
tab1.content:
• Cucumber is a refreshing vegetable with high water content.
  • It has a mild taste.
  • Used in salads and juices.
  • Helps in hydration.
  • Popular worldwide.

tab1.title: Details
tab1.content:
• Green papaya is the unripe form of papaya fruit.
  • It is used as a vegetable in cooking.
  • Common in salads and curries.
  • Contains digestive enzymes.
  • Popular in Asian cuisine.

tab1.title: Details
tab1.content:
• Ivy gourd is a small green vegetable used in Indian cooking.
  • It has a slightly crunchy texture.
  • Used in stir-fries and curries.
  • It is nutritious and easy to cook.
  • Common in South Asian cuisine.

tab1.title: Details
tab1.content:
• Kakrol is a spiny fruit vegetable used in Indian cuisine.
  • It has a unique appearance and texture.
  • Used in curries and fried dishes.
  • Rich in nutrients.
  • Popular in regional dishes.

tab1.title: Details
tab1.content:
• Kundru is a small green vegetable commonly used in Indian cooking.
  • It has a mild taste and crunchy texture.
  • Used in stir-fries and curries.
  • Highly nutritious.
  • Popular in South Asian cuisine.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: • Also called ivy gourd in some regions.

tab1.title: Details
tab1.content:
• Okra is a green vegetable known for its slimy texture when cooked.
  • Used in curries, soups, and fried dishes.
  • It has a mild flavor.
  • Popular in many cuisines worldwide.
  • Also called lady’s finger.

tab1.title: Details
tab1.content:
• Parwal is a small green vegetable widely used in Indian cuisine.
  • It has a mild flavor.
  • Used in curries and stuffed dishes.
  • Easy to cook and digest.
  • Popular in North India.

tab1.title: Details
tab1.content:
• Pumpkin is a large fruit vegetable with bright orange flesh.
  • It has a sweet taste.
  • Used in soups, curries, and desserts.
  • Highly nutritious.
  • Popular worldwide.

tab1.title: Details
tab1.content:
• Ridge gourd is a green vegetable with ridged skin.
  • It has a soft texture when cooked.
  • Used in curries and chutneys.
  • Light and easy to digest.
  • Common in Indian cooking.

tab1.title: Details
tab1.content:
• Snake gourd is a long, slender vegetable.
  • It has a mild taste.
  • Used in curries and stir-fries.
  • Easy to digest.
  • Common in South Indian cuisine.

tab1.title: Details
tab1.content:
• Spaghetti squash is a unique vegetable with stringy flesh.
  • When cooked, it resembles spaghetti.
  • Used as a pasta substitute.
  • Low in calories.
  • Popular in healthy diets.

tab1.title: Details
tab1.content:
• Tinda is a small round vegetable commonly used in Indian cooking.
  • It has a mild taste.
  • Used in curries and stews.
  • Easy to digest.
  • Popular in North India.

tab1.title: Details
tab1.content:
• Tomato is one of the most widely used fruit vegetables.
  • It has a juicy texture and tangy flavor.
  • Used in sauces, salads, and curries.
  • Rich in antioxidants.
  • Essential in global cuisines.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: • Technically a fruit but used as a vegetable.

tab1.title: Details
tab1.content:
• Zucchini is a soft-skinned vegetable similar to cucumber.
  • It has a mild flavor.
  • Used in stir-fries, soups, and grilled dishes.
  • Low in calories.
  • Popular in Mediterranean cuisine.
